name: Caesar Salad#2
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Olive oil - extra-virgin or pure
2 cups 1/2-inch bread cubes - made from 2-day-old rolls, 4 lg slices French bread, or 1/3 baguette
3 Heads romaine - or enough to yield 4 quarts when torn into 2-inch pieces
1/2 cup Parmesan cheese - freshly grated OR Romano cheese OR a combination
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the dressing, whisk together vinegar, anchovy (if desired), mustard, minced garlic, and black pepper.
2. Slowly whisk in 1 cup olive oil, drop by drop at first.
3. When all the oil has been added, whisk in lemon juice and Worcestershire sauce. If omitting the anchovy, season with salt if desired.
4. To prepare croutons, heat olive oil and vegetable oil in a skillet. Saute sliced garlic until lightly browned, about 3 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon.
5. Add bread to the oil and cook over medium heat, stirring and flipping pieces often, until golden on all sides. Remove croutons and drain on several layers of paper towels.
6. To make the salad, remove and discard outer green leaves of romaine. Tear the remaining leaves into 2-inch pieces; rinse and thoroughly dry.
7. Assemble the salad by placing torn romaine in a large bowl. Sprinkle with 1/4 cup cheese. Add croutons and toss well.
8. Add remaining 1/4 cup cheese and toss again. Pour dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine. Serve immediately. Makes 8 first-course servings.
